title: Base58Check Encoding
description: Bitcoin's Base58Check encoding represents versioned payloads such as legacy addresses and WIF keys with a human-friendly alphabet and checksum. The format includes network-specific version bytes, preserves leading zero bytes, and detects many transcription errors.

resources.title: Bitcoin Wiki: Base58Check encoding
resources.notes: Detailed explanation of the alphabet, version bytes, checksum, and leading-zero handling.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Base58Check Encoding
resources.notes: Canonical developer reference for addresses and WIF serialization.

resources.title: Programming Bitcoin
resources.notes: Hands-on treatment of Base58 encoding, checksums, addresses, and key serialization.
